notes: ADR-0.0.16 line 32 explicitly authorizes OBPI-02 as the gate-integration step wiring the OBPI-01 validator into gz gates and consuming OBPI-05's STATUS_VOCAB_MAPPING. Both prerequisites (OBPI-01, OBPI-05) are attested_completed per gz adr status ADR-0.0.16. Plan's scope amendment adds features/gates.feature — not in brief's Allowed Paths — because Heavy-lane Gate 4 (BDD) requires @REQ-* scenario tags per .claude/rules/tests.md GHI #185; this is scope-honest per the OBPI-0.25.0-33 precedent. Six REQs map to eight @covers-decorated tests. Exit-code routing uses existing EXIT_POLICY_BREACH constant from src/gzkit/cli/helpers/exit_codes.py:21. Gate-local renderer chosen over validator's private _render_frontmatter_explain to avoid cross-command private-API coupling. Two tracked defects to file at completion: (1) docs/user/manpages/gates.md does not exist — CLI Doctrine prescribes one for heavy-lane contract changes but brief did not scope creation; (2) gz gates is deprecated — frontmatter guard must migrate to gz closeout Stage 1 eventually, breadcrumb placed in renderer docstring. Plan-before-exploration advisory (Step 6a): plan mode was entered after substantial pre-plan exploration via 3 Explore agents + 1 Plan agent; destination-in-mind was 'wire _validate_frontmatter_coherence into _run_gate_1 with tri-state Literal return and EXIT_POLICY_BREACH routing'; rejected alternatives include reusing validator's private renderer, introducing Gate1Result IntEnum, raising PolicyBreachError from gate handler, wiring into Gate 2, creating manpage in-scope, and absolute 1.0s latency bound. The Plan agent corrected two items (reuse _RECOVERY_COMMANDS dict; latency budget 200ms relative to validator baseline not absolute 1.0s) and validated the architectural choices.
